key_insight: Reduced integration with a scaled boundary parametrization lets VEM integrate the weak form analytically with only one integration point per section, while preserving good accuracy at finite strains.
engineering_relevance: This offers a potentially efficient stabilization strategy for large-deformation solid mechanics simulations, especially for hyperelastic and elasto-plastic problems on polygonal or nonstandard meshes.
method: Taylor-expand constitutive quantities about the section center
Use scaled boundary parametrization to obtain analytical weak-form integration
Reduce quadrature to one point per section while replacing conventional VEM stabilization
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated with a nonlinear patch test and multiple large-deformation examples, including compression, hyperelastic anisotropy, and elasto-plastic notch loading.
watch_item: The formulation did not pass the patch test with higher-order shape functions, and performance was better when the physical element closely matched the assumed parent element geometry.
paper_title: Reduced integration with scaled boundary parametrization for virtual elements at finite strains
